items.title: Knowledge Arbitrage: How the Best Founders Exploit Information Advantages.
items.abstract: The foundational thesis. Why the edge in startups increasingly comes from time-bound, context-dependent insight the market hasn't priced in yet — and how to build a practice around finding it.

items.title: Vibe Investing: Pattern Recognition in the AI Era.
items.abstract: What changes when LLMs collapse the cost of analysis and every investor has the same research tools. Why taste, conviction, and founder chemistry matter more, not less.

items.title: The Skills Layer: What AI Commoditizes and What It Amplifies.
items.abstract: Codified knowledge is becoming free; judgment, taste, and relationships are not. A framework for deciding which skills to invest in and which to let the machines eat.
items.audience: Executive teams, university programs, future-of-work audiences.

items.title: Evaluating Founders When Code Is Cheap.
items.abstract: What early-stage diligence looks like when any team can ship an MVP in a weekend. How to separate signal from noise in a market flooded with demos.
items.audience: Angel groups, VC platforms, corporate venture.

items.title: Teaching Startups at Stanford: What Founders Actually Need to Learn.
items.abstract: Drawn from GSB Startup Garage. Which parts of the canonical startup curriculum hold up, which are obsolete, and what gets added when you have AI-native students.
items.audience: University programs, corporate L&D, education conferences.
